title: Creational - Singleton
content:
# OOP Interview Prep

## Q1: Explain Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP) and show how violating it breaks client applications.

### Standard Answer
LSP states that objects of a superclass should be replaceable with objects of a subclass without affecting application correctness. If a subclass overrides a base class method to throw `UnsupportedOperationException`, it violates LSP. The client, expecting the base class behavior, will crash at runtime because of the unexpected exception.


## Q2: What is the spatial complexity bounds of Creational - Singleton?

### Standard Answer
The spatial complexity scales strictly as $O(1)$ auxiliary memory unless additional arrays or dynamic heap nodes are allocated, yielding $O(N)$ total space.
